description:
You are a planetarium educator preparing a visual showcase called 'Jewels of the Night Sky'. Capture images of five iconic Messier objects using KStars/INDI and organize them into an HTML gallery.

Targets:
- M42 (Orion Nebula): RA 5.5881, Dec -5.3911, Palette: hubble
- M31 (Andromeda Galaxy): RA 0.7122, Dec +41.2692, Palette: enhanced
- M1 (Crab Nebula): RA 5.5756, Dec +22.0144, Palette: narrowband
- M57 (Ring Nebula): RA 18.8931, Dec +33.0292, Palette: vibrant
- M51 (Whirlpool Galaxy): RA 13.4981, Dec +47.1953, Palette: cool

For each target, capture at least three 10-second LIGHT frames and a false-color sky view image using the specified palette via `bash ~/capture_sky_view.sh /home/ga/Outreach/<M-designation>/sky_view.png 0.5 --palette <palette>`. All target files must be stored in their respective directories at `/home/ga/Outreach/<M-designation>/`.

Create the final HTML gallery at `/home/ga/Outreach/gallery.html` with the title 'Jewels of the Night Sky'. The gallery must include the Messier designation, common name, and a reference to the 'sky_view.png' image for each of the five objects.
